title: Multiplexed immunofluorescence and RNA-FISH
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8dcbk","text":"This protocol describes a multiplexed immunofluorescence and RNA-FISH method to detect Atp13a4 mRNA in astrocytes of the visual cortex at postnatal stages. Brain sections from P7, P14, and P21 Aldh1L1-EGFP mice are immunostained with anti-GFP antibodies to label astrocytes and hybridized with custom Atp13a4 probes. Amplification is performed using hairpins specific to the probes and initiator-labeled antibodies. Sections are imaged using high-resolution confocal microscopy, and Atp13a4 puncta are quantified within GFP-positive astrocyte regions using a FIJI-based custom pipeline. This approach enables spatially resolved analysis of Atp13a4 expression during postnatal development.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"fh5hk","text":"Follow institutional guidelines for the disposal of biological and chemical waste.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"dfsfk","text":"Custom Atp13a4 probes (Molecular Instruments)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fl2o4","text":"Buffers and reagents (Molecular Instruments)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"35vrj","text":"Primary antibody: anti-GFP (Aves Labs, Cat# GFP-1020, RRID:AB_10000240) ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"70rui","text":"Secondary antibody: Initiator-labeled (specific for GFP detection)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bk2ga","text":"Mounting medium","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6iikg","text":"FIJI software with custom pipeline","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4n6h8","text":"Microscope: Olympus FV 3000","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5qmjk","text":"Other reagents:","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1hjf","text":"1× DPBS with 0.1% Tween20 (PBST)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"308vs","text":"paraformaldehyde (PFA; Electron Microscopy Sciences, Cat# 19210)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"da6bq","text":"5× SSCT (sodium chloride sodium citrate with 0.1% Tween20)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7lv1j","text":"Probe wash buffer","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1bspq","text":"Amplification buffer","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8n3ut","text":"Hairpins specific to initiator-labeled secondary antibody and Atp13a4 probes","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":1,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Prepare five 20 μm brain sections containing the visual cortex from P7, P14, and P21 Aldh1L1-EGFP mice.
Mount sections directly onto glass slides and store at -80 °C until use.
Thaw slides at room temperature for 00:05:00 to remove the cryoprotective solution.
Wash sections three times with 1× PBST.
Incubate sections with anti-GFP primary antibody (1:1,000) Overnight at 4 °C in a humidified chamber.
Block sections with antibody buffer for 01:00:00 at Room temperature .
Wash sections with PBST.
Incubate sections with 100 µL initiator-labeled secondary antibody diluted in antibody buffer for 01:00:00 at Room temperature .
Fix sections with 4% PFA for 00:10:00 .
Wash sections with 5× sodium chloride sodium citrate containing 0.1% Tween20 (5× SSCT) for 00:05:00 .
Incubate sections with 100 µL of 16 nM Atp13a4 probe solution Overnight at 37 °C in a humidified chamber.
Remove excess probes by washing sections in a mixture of probe wash buffer and 5× SSCT at 37 °C .
Gradually increase the concentration of 5× SSCT during washes, ending with a 100% 5× SSCT wash.
Add 200 µL amplification buffer to each section and incubate at Room temperature for 00:30:00 .
Incubate sections with the hairpin solution Overnight at Room temperature .
Mix snap-cooled hairpins specific to the initiator-labeled secondary antibody and Atp13a4 probe with amplification buffer to create a 60 mM hairpin solution.
Wash sections with 5× SSCT for 00:45:00 to remove excess hairpins.
Dry sections and mount with mounting medium.
Image sections within two days using an Olympus FV 3000 microscope at high magnification (60× objective with 2× optical zoom).
Acquire z-stack images (1 μm step size, 6 μm total z-stack depth) for the visual cortex (layers L1, L2-3, L4, and L5).
Capture images from three sections per animal for three animals at each time point (e.g. P7, P14, and P21).
Process images using a FIJI custom pipeline.
Threshold and quantify Atp13a4 puncta inside and outside GFP-positive astrocyte areas.
Analyze data using software available at https://github.com/Eroglu-Lab/Irala_2024_image_analysis/blob/main/20230717_irala_fish_macro.ijm.
creator: Sarah van Veen
